channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 and Tier 2 sources

item.title: 🇱🇧✈️ Lebanon Opens Second International Airport Amid Israel-Hezbollah War
item.description: Lebanon inaugurated Qlayaat airport near the Syrian border as a second international hub, converting it from military airbase to passenger use as the country seeks to shift traffic from Beirut amid the ongoing conflict.

item.title: 🇮🇳🎓 India Youth March Against Exam Leaks in Delhi Cockroaches Protest
item.description: Thousands of young Indians protested in New Delhi under the angry cockroaches movement, demanding accountability for exam paper leaks that have undermined educational opportunities and government job prospects.

item.title: 🇸🇬🏛️ Singapore Blocks Posts Targeting Indian Community Over Racial Harmony
item.description: Singapore blocked access to 14 social media posts containing inflammatory claims against its Indian community, citing concerns over racial harmony and social cohesion.

item.title: 🛢️💰 US Companies Benefit From Strait of Hormuz Disruptions, Rosneft Chief Says
item.description: Igor Sechin claimed American energy companies are profiting from disruptions to the key shipping route, while China is better prepared than other nations for potential supply interruptions.

item.title: 🇵🇸🏛️ Hamas Says Cairo Talks Begin on Gaza Ceasefire Implementation
item.description: Hamas announced that talks in Cairo are focusing on ending Israeli violations, opening crossings, delivering aid, and completing the first phase of the ceasefire agreement.

item.title: 🇵🇸 Israeli Strike Kills Five at Wedding in Gaza
item.description: At least five people were killed and others wounded when Israeli forces bombed a wedding tent in Gaza City. The attack comes amid ongoing conflict in the region.

item.title: 🇯🇲 Jamaica Experiences Island-Wide Power Blackout
item.description: Jamaica is experiencing a nationwide power outage, with the energy minister confirming the blackout on social media. No cause has been given yet.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Job Market Rebounds Despite Iran War Costs
item.description: US employers added 172,000 jobs in May as the labor market showed resilience despite rising costs from the Iran conflict. The labor market continues to recover.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump Urges Firing of Intelligence Community Employees
item.description: President Trump told the Wall Street Journal he wants the acting director of national intelligence to begin firing large numbers of employees as part of a shake-up of the US intelligence community.

item.title: 🤖 Meta Considers Major Stock Offering for AI Infrastructure
item.description: Meta is weighing raising tens of billions of dollars in a stock offering to fund AI infrastructure ambitions, according to Financial Times. The move comes as tech giants race to expand AI capabilities.

item.title: 🇵🇸🇮🇱 Israeli forces kill Palestinian infant in West Bank shooting
item.description: A seven-month-old Palestinian baby was killed and his parents wounded when Israeli soldiers opened fire on their vehicle in Hebron. The Israeli military said the incident is under review and those injured were uninvolved civilians.

item.title: 🇲🇲💥 Explosion at Myanmar building storing mining explosives kills over 45
item.description: A blast at a building in northeastern Myanmar near the Chinese border killed more than 45 people and injured 70 others. The Taang National Liberation Army said gelignite stored for mining operations detonated.

item.title: 🇳🇪💀 At least 49 migrants die of thirst in Niger desert after truck breakdown
item.description: Forty-nine people died of thirst in the Sahara desert after their truck broke down while traveling from Mali to Niger for Eid al-Adha celebrations. Two survivors walked more than 50 km to alert authorities.

item.title: 🇹🇼🇨🇳🌊 Taiwan accuses China of coordinated operation near South China Sea outpost
item.description: Taiwan's coast guard accused China of attempting to create a 'false illusion' of jurisdiction over waters surrounding a strategic South China Sea outpost, as a Chinese coast guard ship and survey vessel carried out coordinated operations.

item.title: 🇷🇺🔥🛢️ Major fire breaks out at one of Russias biggest oil refineries
item.description: A large blaze broke out at the Antipinsky Oil Refinery in Tyumen, one of Russias biggest private refineries, according to local reports.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Lebanon Accuses Iran of Using Country as Bargaining Chip in US Negotiations
item.description: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun made his toughest criticism yet of Tehran, accusing Iran of leveraging Lebanon in its talks with Washington while Hezbollah wages war with Israel. The statement highlights growing Arab frustration with Iranian interference.

item.title: 🇰🇵 North Korea Plans 10,000-Ton Destroyer and Secret Underwater Weapons
item.description: State media announced plans for a large destroyer and nuclear-armed naval fleet ahead of Chinese President Xi Jinping's visit. Kim Jong Un vowed to accelerate development of naval nuclear capabilities.

item.title: 🇪🇺 Macron, Starmer, Merz to Meet Zelenskiy in London for War Talks
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer, and German Chancellor Friedrich Merz will hold talks with Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskiy on Sunday, June 7, as European leaders seek to support Ukraine amid stalled peace efforts.

item.title: 🛰️ NASA Orders ISS Astronauts to Shelter During Russian Module Air Leak Repair
item.description: Five astronauts aboard the International Space Station took shelter and prepared for evacuation as Russian cosmonauts attempted to fix a worsening crack in the Zvezda service module. NASA later reversed the evacuation order after repairs.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran demands $24 billion in frozen assets as condition for US peace deal progress
item.description: Iranian military adviser Mohsen Rezaei said negotiations with the US have reached a deadlock, tying progress to the release of $24 billion in frozen Iranian assets. Tensions remain high following US strikes on Iranian coastal sites.

item.title: 🐼🇹🇼 Taipei Zoo Welcomes First Red Pandas From China in Over a Decade
item.description: Taiwan received red pandas from a zoo in Fujian province, marking a rare instance of animal diplomacy amid cross-strait tensions and the first such exchange since 2014.

item.title: 🇧🇭🇰🇼 Iran Fires Missiles at Bahrain and Kuwait, Escalating Regional Conflict
item.description: Iran launched ballistic missiles and drones at Bahrain and Kuwait on Saturday, with Bahrain intercepting the attacks. Kuwait condemned the strikes as a dangerous escalation threatening Gulf stability.

item.title: 🇦🇲 Armenia Holds Election That Could Reshape Ties With Russia and the West
item.description: Armenia votes Sunday in parliamentary elections that could pivot the country away from Russia toward closer Western ties, with Prime Minister Pashinyan seeking a mandate for constitutional reforms.

item.title: ⚔️ US Strikes Iranian Radar Sites After Drone Launch Toward Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US forces attacked Iranian coastal radar installations on Saturday after intercepting drones launched by Iran toward the strategic Strait of Hormuz, marking the latest escalation in the ongoing conflict.

item.title: 🇺🇦🤝 Ukraine and Russia Exchange 185 Prisoners Each in Latest POW Swap
item.description: Ukraine and Russia each exchanged 185 service personnel on Friday in the latest prisoner of war swap, continuing a pattern of exchanges amid the ongoing conflict.
